recalls.Description: The DIY Sauna Heater Kit is under recall due to potential fire hazards that could lead to serious injury or death. This kit allows consumers to construct a heating fixture with four separate heat-lamp bulbs and a metal enclosure designed for use in a DIY sauna, for which carpentry plans are also available. The kit includes a stainless steel cage weighing approximately 16 lbs, measuring 12"H x 12"W x 8"D when assembled, along with necessary assembly hardware and a three-prong power cord. It is meant to be attached to a custom-built wood frame.
recalls.Title: DIY Cold Plunge Recalls Sauna Heater Kit Due to Fire Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact DIY Cold Plunge via email at orders@diycoldplunge.com or visit www.diycoldplunge.com/pages/recall. You can also click on 'Recall Information' at the bottom of the homepage.

recalls.Description: Wagner is recalling its 900 Series power steamers due to a serious burn hazard. This recall affects the models 905e Auto Steamer, 915e On-Demand Power Steamer, and 925e Steam Machine Elite Steamer. Each model features a yellow and black pressurized boiler base marked with 'Wagner' and includes various accessories. The steamer base is equipped with a clear storage compartment, a handle, wheels, an eight-foot steam hose, and a nozzle with a trigger for steam control.
recalls.Title: Wagner 900 Series Power Steamers Recalled Due to Burn Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled steamers and reach out to Wagner at 800 962-6118 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also email us-wagnerrecall@wagner-group.com or visit www.wagnerspraytech.com/900-series-recall for further assistance.

recalls.Description:
LShome has issued a recall for their 3-Pack Smoke Detector Fire Alarms due to a critical safety issue. These alarms, which are powered by 9V batteries, feature a light sound warning and a test button. They are circular and white in design, with the model number 'XG-7D04-KZ9Z' and SKU 'CX-50YP-A5VN' located on the bottom of the unit. The alarms may not activate in a timely manner if the sensing threshold for security warnings is improperly set, creating a significant fire hazard.

Consumers are advised to stop using these smoke detectors immediately. For a full refund, please reach out via email to lmm15957491237@163.com. The smoke alarms can be disposed of in regular household trash. These products were sold exclusively online at Amazon.com from February 2024 through December 2025 for approximately $30.
recalls.Title: LShome Smoke Detector Fire Alarms Recalled Due to Failure to Alert During Fires, Risking Serious Injury or Death
recalls.ConsumerContact: Email lmm15957491237@163.com for refund instructions.

recalls.Description:
KingPavonini has issued a recall for six models of their adult portable bed rails due to serious safety concerns. These bed rails can pose a risk of entrapment and asphyxiation when attached to a bed, as users may become trapped between the rail and the mattress. The recalled models feature white or black tubing with rounded black foam grips and may include support legs and motion-sensor lights. The brand name 'KingPavonini' is displayed on the fabric cover of the rails.

The affected models include BC-CBJ-X, BC-CBJ, BC-LDFS-X, LDFS-SSK, 04-BC-LDFS, and BC-LDFS, with varying dimensions and features. Consumers are urged to stop using these bed rails immediately and follow the provided disposal instructions to ensure safety.

recalls.Description:
IBC Technologies has issued a recall for its CX Combi Boilers, which are compact residential gas boilers designed for central heating and on-demand hot water. The affected models, CX-199 and CX-150, may pose a burn risk as the water temperature can exceed the control panel settings. This recall affects specific serial numbers from units manufactured between April 2023 and April 2025, as well as those produced from June 2024 to April 2025. The model and serial numbers can be found on the touch screen and rating plate inside the unit.

Consumers who own these boilers should check their model and serial numbers against the recall list to determine if their unit is affected. IBC Technologies is offering a free in-home repair service to install a mixing valve if necessary, allowing consumers to continue using their boilers for heating while ensuring safety.

HTP has issued a recall for its ELU Elite Ultra residential wall-mounted gas boilers, specifically the models ELU-150WBN and ELU-199WBN. These boilers, which are black and approximately 20 inches wide and 34 inches high, may pose serious hazards due to a potential failure of the boiler door to seal properly. This defect can lead to the escape of flames and harmful gases, creating risks of fire and carbon monoxide poisoning, which could result in severe injury or death.

Consumers are advised to check the rating plate on the left side of the boiler for the model and serial numbers. recalls.Description:
Midea is recalling approximately 1.7 million U and U+ Window Air Conditioners due to a potential mold exposure hazard. These air conditioners, which fit into windows, were sold under various brand names including Midea, Comfort Aire, Danby, Frigidaire, Insignia, Keystone, LBG Products, Mr. Cool, Perfect Aire, and Sea Breeze. They are available in three cooling capacities: 8,000, 10,000, and 12,000 BTU, and are identifiable by their white color and specific model numbers located on a label on the front right side of the unit.

The issue arises from pooled water that may not drain efficiently, leading to mold growth that can cause respiratory problems or infections for some users. Consumers are advised to check the model numbers associated with this recall and take immediate action if they own one of the affected units.
recalls.Title: Midea Recalls 1.7 Million U and U+ Window Air Conditioners Over Mold Risk

BASDEHEN kitchen faucets, specifically model CFDTTH-000-YGH, are being recalled due to dangerous levels of lead that can leach into drinking water, posing a significant health risk to infants and young children. These faucets feature a matte black finish, a single handle, a 360° swivel, a spring spout design, and a detachable spray. Consumers can check their Amazon purchase history for the terms 'Basdehen' and 'kitchen faucet' to determine if they own a recalled model.

Lead exposure can lead to serious neurological issues in young children, including behavioral problems and reduced cognitive abilities. Immediate action is required to ensure the safety of children in households with these products.

recalls.Description: Academy Sports + Outdoors has announced a recall of their Redfield 12-Gun and 18-Gun Fireproof Safes due to a significant safety concern. The 12-Gun Safe measures 53 inches high, 15 inches wide, and 16 inches deep, while the 18-Gun Safe measures 55 inches high, 18 inches wide, and 18 inches deep. Both models feature 'Redfield' branding on the front door above the keypad, with model and serial numbers located on a red label at the bottom right corner of the door.

recalls.Description: New Cosmos USA is recalling the DeNova Detect combination natural gas and carbon monoxide alarms, model DD622NCV. To check if your unit is affected by this recall, please visit www.goodmanmfg.com/package-recall.
recalls.Title: Daikin Comfort Technologies Recalls Amana, Daikin, and Goodman Air Conditioning and Heat Pump Units Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact DCT toll-free at 833-324-5460 between 8 a.m. and 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday, or visit www.goodmanmfg.com/package-recall for further details.

Products.Name: Amana, Daikin, and Goodman Packaged Air Conditioning and Heat Pump Units

Images.Caption: Recalled Goodman branded air conditioning and heat pump
Recalled air conditioning and heat pump model number label location

Injuries.Name: None reported

Manufacturers.Name: Daikin Comfort Technologies Manufacturing L.P. (f/k/a Goodman Manufacturing Company L.P.), of Houston, Texas

Retailers.Name: Independent heating and cooling equipment dealers across the nation, sold from January 2024 to March 2024, priced between $4,500 and $7,500.

Hazards.Name: Incorrect electrical ratings on the unit's serial plate, posing a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should refrain from opening the outside electrical panel and should immediately reach out to Daikin Comfort Technologies for a complimentary repair and inspection. DCT will also contact all known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Daikin recalls Amana, Daikin, and Goodman air conditioning units due to fire hazards. Check if your unit is affected and learn how to get help.

recalls.Description: SA Consumer Products is recalling the Sanctuary Quick Access Biometric Home Safe and the Sports Afield Home Defense 4-Gun Biometric Safe due to a significant safety concern. These black steel safes are designed for storing firearms and valuables. The Sanctuary safe measures 10 inches in height, 14 inches in width, and 10 inches in depth, while the Sports Afield safe is larger, measuring 55 inches high, 12 inches wide, and 11 inches deep. The Sanctuary model accommodates approximately two firearms, whereas the Sports Afield model can hold up to four firearms and features the Sports Afield logo prominently on its front.
recalls.Title: SA Consumer Products Recalls Sanctuary Quick Access and Sports Afield Biometric Gun Safes Due to Serious Injury and Death Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact SA Consumer Products at 855-215-5822 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, reach out via email at saconsumer6983@sedgwick.com or visit sabiometricsaferecall.expertinquiry.com or www.saproducts.com and click on the 'Recall' tab for further details.

Products.Name: Sanctuary Quick Access Biometric Home Safe

Images.Caption: Recalled Sanctuary Quick Access Biometric Home Safe
Recalled Sports Afield Home Defense 4-Gun Biometric Safe

Injuries.Name: The firm is aware of 77 reports of the safe being opened by unauthorized users. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Costco and other retailers nationwide, as well as online at Costco.com, priced between $75 and $100. The Sports Afield safe was sold at firearm and sporting goods stores and online for $500 to $600. Sales occurred from July 2020 through November 2023.

Importers.Name: SA Consumer Products Inc., of Rolling Meadows, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Unauthorized access to the biometric lock can lead to serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease using the biometric feature, remove the batteries, and use the key for firearm storage. Contact SA Consumer Products for a free repair kit.

recalls.metaDescription: SA Consumer Products recalls Sanctuary and Sports Afield biometric safes due to serious safety risks. Learn how to get a free repair kit.

recalls.Description: Compare Brands is recalling its ADIOS! Super Vinegar All Natural Cleaner, a 30% concentrate product. The cleaner is packaged in a 16 oz. white bottle with a blue cap and features front labeling that states, 'ADIOS! SUPER VINEGAR ALL NATURAL CLEANER. 30% Concentrate Indoor & Outdoor use.' The back label promotes the product's natural cleaning power but lacks essential safety warnings, leading to potential risks of poisoning and chemical burns.
recalls.Title: Compare Brands Recalls ADIOS! Super Vinegar All Natural Cleaner Due to Poisoning and Chemical Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Compare Brands toll-free at 888-912-4622 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email support@comparebrands.net. More information is available at https://comparebrands.net/pages/product-recalls or by visiting https://comparebrands.net/ and clicking 'Recall' at the top of the page.

recalls.Description: Kano Laboratories has announced a recall of several Super Lube products, including the Syncopen Synthetic Penetrant, Fire Resistant Hydraulic Fluid, and Metal Protectant and Corrosion Inhibitor. These items are designed to prevent corrosion in machinery and are sold in various container sizes. The packaging does not meet child-resistant standards, posing a significant risk of poisoning to young children if ingested. Additionally, the products contain substances that may lead to serious respiratory issues if inhaled.
recalls.Title: Kano Laboratories Issues Recall for Super Lube® Products Due to Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to keep the recalled Super Lube® products out of children's reach and contact Kano Laboratories for a full refund. Proof of possession is required, which can be submitted by emailing a photo of the product to info@super-lube.com. For further assistance, call 800-253-5823 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.super-lube.com/recall.

Images.Caption: Front Labels of Recalled Super Lube Syncopen Synthetic Penetrant
Rear Labels of Recalled Super Lube Syncopen Synthetic Penetrant

Injuries.Name: None reported

Manufacturers.Name: Kano Laboratories LLC, of Bohemia, New York

Retailers.Name: Available at Grainger Parts, E & R Industrial Sales, Home Depot, and various retailers nationwide, as well as online at Amazon.com and other websites.

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging and potential respiratory harm from inhalation.

Remedies.Name: Immediate removal of products from children's reach and contact for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Kano Laboratories recalls Super Lube® products due to poisoning risk. Ensure safety by securing these items and contacting for a refund.

These rectangular rugs are gray, measuring 8 feet by 10 feet, and feature a label stating "100% Polyester" and "Made in China" on the underside. Consumers are urged to take immediate action regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: Touchat Large Fuzzy Area Rugs Recalled for Fire Hazard Violation
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Touchat at 909-390-0801 from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at jurysun@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Touchat Large Fuzzy Area Rug

Distributors.Name: Jian De Shi Jia Jin Chu Kou You Xian Gong Si DBA Touchat, of China

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to violation of federal flammability regulations

recalls.Description: Empower Brands has announced an expanded recall affecting all BLACK+DECKER Model HGS011 Easy Garment Steamers, including those previously repaired in November 2022. These steamers, available in various colors, feature the BLACK+DECKER name at the base and measure approximately 11 inches in height and 6 inches in width. Model numbers can be found on the bottom of the steamer and the packaging, with UPC codes located on the sides, back, and bottom of the color package.
recalls.Title: Major Recall of Two Million BLACK+DECKER Garment Steamers Due to Burn Hazard; Refund Available
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Empower Brands at 800-990-5298 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email hgsrecall@brandprotectplus.com, or visit www.prodprotect.com/recall or www.blackanddeckerappliances.com and click on 'Safety Notices' for further information.

Products.Name: BLACK+DECKER HGS011 Easy Garment Steamer
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1.6 million (518,500 units were previously recalled in November 2022)

Injuries.Name: Since the recall was announced in November 2022, Empower Brands has received 317 reports of hot water expelling from the recalled steamers, including 82 reports of burn injuries, seven of which were second-degree burns. 94 of these incident reports involved units that were repaired as part of the original recall or models featuring the updated design, including 19 burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart, Target, Amazon, Bed Bath & Beyond, and other retailers nationwide, including online at www.walmart.com, www.target.com, www.amazon.com, www.bedbathandbeyond.com, and www.blackanddeckerappliances.com from June 2021 to February 2024, priced between $14 and $23.

Importers.Name: Spectrum Brands Inc., of Middleton, Wis., owns subsidiary Empower Brands LLC

Hazards.Name: Risk of burns due to expulsion of hot water during use.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled garment steamers and contact Empower Brands for a full refund. Reports of burn injuries have continued, including those involving units repaired in the previous recall. The remedy has now been updated to a refund for all units, including those that received a replacement upper assembly.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of BLACK+DECKER garment steamers due to burn risk; full refunds offered. Contact Empower Brands for more information.

recalls.Description: Crown Boiler Company is recalling its Aruba IV (AWR Series) gas-fired hot water boilers that are designed for natural gas and propane use at altitudes exceeding 2,000 feet. The affected boilers are identifiable by their red and gray color scheme and a height of 39.5 inches. The model name Aruba IV is not labeled directly on the unit, but the model number can be found on the rating plate located on the right side of the boiler. This recall includes various model numbers, all manufactured regardless of the date.

recalls.Description: U.S. Boiler Company is recalling several models of gas-fired residential hot water boilers due to a potential carbon monoxide poisoning hazard. This recall affects models from U.S. Boiler, New Yorker Boiler, Advantage, FORCE, and Archer that are installed at elevations above 5,400 feet. Notable models include the U.S. Boiler ES2, Series 3, X-2, Lancer, and FORCE models, each identifiable by specific color and logo placements. The model and serial numbers can be found on the rating plate located on the boiler's top panel or inside the vestibule behind a removable door.

recalls.Description: This recall affects various handheld garment steamers from three brands: Steamfast (models SF-425, SF-435, SF-440, SF-445, SF-447), Vornado (model VS-410), and Sharper Image (model SI-428). The steamers were available in multiple colors, including white, black, light pink, hot pink, sage green, orange, and teal blue. These appliances, designed to eliminate wrinkles from fabrics using hot steam, have the brand name and model number labeled on the unit. Consumers are advised to check their steamers against the recall list.
recalls.Title: Vornado Recalls Two Million Handheld Garment Steamers Due to Burn Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Vornado at 888-240-2768 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.vornado.com/recalls/hhgs. recalls.Description: BISSELL is recalling its Multi Reach Hand and Floor Vacuum Cleaners, including models 1985, 19851 (also known as Multi Auto), 19859, 1985T, 2151, 21512, 21513, 21517, 21518, 21519, 2151A, 2151T, 2151W, and 2151V. The product features 'Multi Reach' on the hand vacuum part and comes in various colors. Model numbers can be found on the rating label located behind the dirt tank. It is important to note that recalled lithium-ion batteries must be disposed of according to local regulations and should not be thrown in the trash or placed in used battery recycling bins at retail locations.

recalls.Description:
Daikin Comfort Technologies Manufacturing (formerly Goodman Manufacturing Company L.P.) has expanded its recall to include additional evaporator coil drain pans manufactured between January 2019 and February 2020. These units were incorrectly labeled with a 2018 serial number. The recall now encompasses approximately 3,600 more units with specific configurations and serial numbers. Consumers should verify their units against the provided serial number ranges to determine if they are affected by this recall.

The molded plastic drain pan at the bottom of the evaporator coil poses a fire risk as it can overheat, melt, and deform. Consumers who have had their furnace systems repaired with a new evaporator coil drain pan during the recall period should also check if their units are included. For assistance, Daikin offers a model verification tool available at https://www.recallrtr.com/drainpan.
recalls.Title: Daikin Comfort Technologies Expands Recall of Evaporator Coil Drain Pans Due to Fire Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach DCT toll-free at 888-520-0579 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.recallrtr.com/drainpan or www.goodmanmfg.com and click on 'PRODUCT RECALL'.

Products.Name: Evaporator Coil Drain Pans
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 3,600 additional units (In addition, about 12 were sold in Canada) (298,300 evaporator coil drain pans, and 44,000 in Canada, were previously recalled in November 2021 and in July 2022)

Injuries.Name: DCT has received a total of 31 reports of fire involving the drain pans, including five reports since the recall was reannounced in July 2022. The incidents resulted in one reported smoke inhalation and several reports of significant fire and smoke damage to residences.

Manufacturers.Name: Daikin Comfort Technologies Manufacturing L.P. (f/k/a Goodman Manufacturing Company L.P.) of Houston, Texas

Retailers.Name: Independent Heating and Cooling Equipment Dealers Nationwide

Hazards.Name: Fire Hazard due to Overheating and Melting

Remedies.Name: Free Repair by a Qualified Technician

recalls.metaDescription: Daikin Comfort Technologies recalls additional evaporator coil drain pans due to fire hazards. Check if your unit is affected and get a free repair.

recalls.Description: Alliance4Safety, in collaboration with 31 furniture manufacturers, has issued a recall for New Age furniture tip-over restraint kits. These kits, designed to secure clothing storage units, were sold alongside furniture produced in Vietnam. The kits consist of a plastic zip-tie, two brackets, and two screws, and can pose serious risks if they fail during a tip-over incident. The manufacturing date is indicated on a sticker or stamp, which should read November 2019 or later. The packaging features white with black text and includes instructions for anchoring furniture, stating 'Manufactured by New Age Industries.'
recalls.Title: Recall of New Age Furniture Tip-over Restraint Kits by Alliance4Safety and 31 Furniture Brands Due to Child Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Alliance4Safety at 855-416-7370 or visit www.alliance4safety.org/new-age-recall for further details.

Products.Name: New Age Furniture Tip-over Restraint Kits

Images.Caption: Recalled New Age tip restraint kit with plastic zip-tie, 2 brackets, and 2 screws
Packaging containing the recalled New Age tip restraint kits, stating "Manufactured by New Age Industries" at the bottom

Injuries.Name: Furniture companies have received two reports of the New Age tip kits breaking. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: New Age Industries Co. LTD, of Vietnam

Retailers.Name: Available at furniture retailers across the nation and online since November 2019

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment due to brittle plastic zip ties

Remedies.Name: Free replacement tip kit for affected customers

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: New Age furniture tip-over restraint kits pose serious risks to children. Check for safety and contact Alliance4Safety for replacements.

recalls.Description: Klein Tools and Blackfire are recalling their portable rechargeable power stations due to potential fire and burn hazards. These power stations, featuring lithium-ion batteries, can deliver up to 1500W of AC power and include a built-in 983Wh battery pack. They are equipped with four 120V outlets, six USB ports, and a 12V car port. The model identifiers are PAC1000 for Blackfire and KTB1000 for Klein Tools, both prominently displayed on the rear label. The units are designed in orange and black with 'Klein Tools' in white on the front.
recalls.Title: Klein Tools and Blackfire Power Stations Recalled for Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Klein Tools at 800-527-3099 from 7:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.kleintools.com/recall/ktb1000 for Klein Tools power stations and www.blackfire.com/recall/pac1000 for Blackfire power stations. More details can also be found by navigating to the 'Safety Recalls' section on their websites.

Products.NumberOfUnits: About 3,800 (In addition, about 164 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Klein Tools KTB1000 Portable Rechargeable Power Station
Recalled Blackfire PAC1000 Portable Rechargeable Power Station

Injuries.Name: Klein Tools received four reports of overheating. No injuries have been reported. No lithium-ion battery fires have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through industrial distributors and electrical wholesalers across the nation from November 2021 to November 2023, priced around $1,300.

Importers.Name: Klein Tools, of Lincolnshire, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Overheating of internal inverter boards leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to cease using the recalled power stations and return them to the place of purchase. Klein Tools will reach out to all known buyers.

recalls.metaDescription: Klein Tools and Blackfire recall power stations due to fire risks. Stop using and return to purchase point for safety.

recalls.Description: Pella has issued a recall for its Architect Series venting wood and aluminum-clad casement windows due to a potential injury hazard. This recall affects specific custom-made casement windows, including all venting hurricane impact models with side pivot hardware, those with a frame height exceeding 96 inches, and casements with frame widths greater than 35 inches or vent heights over 47 inches that also feature side pivot hardware. Consumers can verify if their windows are part of this recall by checking the serial number etched in the lower corner of the window glass at www.PellaHingeRecall.expertinquiry.com.
recalls.Title: Pella Recalls Architect Series Casement Windows Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Pella toll-free at 888-276-7624 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. More information is available online at www.pella.com by clicking on 'Recall Notice' or by visiting www.PellaHingeRecall.expertinquiry.com to check if their window serial number is included in the recall.

recalls.Description: Ergodyne is recalling the Squids 3705 Wire Tool Attachments featuring Screw Gate Tool Tails due to safety concerns. These tools are designed to secure equipment and prevent drops while working at heights. The attachments are identifiable by their 5 ¾-inch orange wire loop and a black and green tag that displays 'Ergodyne' and 'Model 3705'. This recall specifically affects model number 3705, which was sold in packs of six or as part of various kits numbered 3170, 3183, 3188, and 3189.
recalls.Title: Ergodyne Recalls Squids 3705 Wire Tool Attachments Due to Potential Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled wire tool attachments immediately and reach out to Ergodyne at 800-522-0520 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday. For further assistance, email returns@ergodyne.com or visit https://www.ergodyne.com/product-recalls for more details.

Products.Name: Squids 3705 Wire Tool Attachments
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 16,570 (In addition, about 790 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Ergodyne Squids 3705 with Model Number

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available through industrial and safety product distributors nationwide and online at https://www.ergodyne.com/ and other online platforms like Amazon.com from September 2018 to July 2023, priced around $20 for a six-pack or up to $400 when included in kits.

Importers.Name: Tenacious Holdings Inc., dba Ergodyne, of St. Paul, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury from wire tool attachment breaking when tethered to tools over two pounds.

recalls.Description: TTI Floor Care has issued a recall for Oreck Discover Upright Vacuums, specifically models UK30500 and UK30500PC. The affected units have serial numbers starting with AJ2226 through AJ2232. You can find the model and serial number on a silver label located on the bottom of the vacuum, which features a silver and black design with the names 'Oreck' and 'Discover' displayed prominently.
recalls.Title: Recall of Oreck Discover Upright Vacuums Due to Risk of Electrical Shock
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Oreck at 888-383-7000 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.oreck.com/pages/recall. Alternatively, go to www.oreck.com and click on 'Product Recall' at the bottom of the page for further information.

Images.Caption: Recalled Oreck Discover Upright Vacuum

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 41 reports of a prong detaching from its plug. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through independent Oreck dealers nationwide and online at www.acmetools.com, www.amazon.com, www.homedepot.com, and www.oreck.com from September 2022 to September 2023, priced at approximately $600.

Importers.Name: Royal Appliance Mfg. Co., d/b/a TTI Floor Care North America, of Charlotte, North Carolina
